skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Guides an AI assistant through a complete implementation plan using controlled task batches, verification steps, and review checkpoints.
skill.summary: Execute implementation plans in reviewed batches with clear checkpoints.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: The two reported high-risk weak cryptography findings are duplicate false positives on SKILL.md line 3. The line is frontmatter description text about executing plans, with no cryptographic algorithm, code execution primitive, network behavior, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The duplicate static findings point to SKILL.md line 3, which is descriptive metadata for the skill workflow. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA1, encryption code, key handling, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line contains only a natural-language description. The full skill file contains process guidance and no executable code or cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Long implementation plans can become hard to control when work happens without checkpoints. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code execute planned tasks in small batches with review and verification.
actual_capabilities: Loads and reviews a complete implementation plan before work begins.
Identifies plan concerns and asks for clarification before execution.
Executes the first three tasks by default as a controlled batch.
Tracks task status from in progress to completed during execution.
Reports implemented work and verification output after each batch.
Stops when blockers, unclear instructions, or repeated verification failures occur.
limitations: Requires a complete implementation plan from the user.
Does not create the plan from scratch.
Depends on another finishing skill for final branch completion.
Does not resolve missing dependencies or unclear requirements without user input.

use_cases.title: Controlled Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Execute a prepared feature plan in small batches with review after each group of tasks.

use_cases.title: Plan-Based Refactoring
use_cases.description: Apply a refactoring plan step by step while preserving verification and feedback points.

use_cases.title: Reviewed Delivery Workflow
use_cases.description: Keep AI-assisted implementation aligned with an architect or reviewer during longer work.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill. Read this implementation plan, review it for concerns, then execute the first batch with verification.

prompt_templates.title: Execute With Checkpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to execute this plan in batches of three tasks. Stop after each batch and report verification results.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Starting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill. First identify gaps, blockers, and unclear instructions in this plan. Start only after concerns are resolved.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Complex Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill for this full delivery plan. Track task state, run specified checks, request review between batches, and stop on unresolved failures.

output_examples.input: Execute the first batch from my completed implementation plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, flags any concerns, creates a task list, completes the first three tasks, runs checks, and asks for feedback.

output_examples.input: Continue after I approved the previous batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies any requested changes, starts the next batch, reports completed work, and includes verification results.

output_examples.input: A verification step failed twice during the batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops execution, explains the blocker, summarizes attempted checks, and asks for clarification instead of guessing.

best_practices: Provide a complete plan with small, ordered tasks and explicit verification steps.
Review each batch report before asking the assistant to continue.
Clarify blockers and failed checks before allowing more implementation work.
anti_patterns: Starting execution from a vague plan without acceptance criteria.
Forcing progress after repeated failures or unclear instructions.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant execute a prepared implementation plan in small batches with review checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it write an implementation plan?
faq.answer: No. It expects the user to provide a complete plan before execution begins.

faq.question: How many tasks are executed at once?
faq.answer: The default batch size is three tasks before reporting and waiting for feedback.

faq.question: What happens when a blocker appears?
faq.answer: The assistant stops, explains the blocker, and asks for clarification instead of continuing.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it perform final branch cleanup?
faq.answer: It directs the assistant to use a separate finishing skill after all planned tasks are complete.
